description: Mobile-first web app for managing ralph-teams loops. Users authenticate, connect GitHub repos as projects, run epics via a dashboard, and chat with an AI assistant (opencode SDK + MCP) that can operate ralph-teams on their behalf. Stack: Next.js 15 App Router, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Supabase (auth + Postgres + Realtime), Runner service (Express on Node), opencode SDK for chat, MCP server wrapping ralph-teams CLI.

epics.title: MCP Server
epics.description: Standalone stdio-based MCP server that wraps ralph-teams CLI commands as tools. The opencode server on the runner uses this to give the AI assistant the ability to operate ralph-teams. Lives in mcp-server/ directory. Uses @modelcontextprotocol/sdk. Each tool receives PROJECT_PATH from env and scopes all operations to that directory.

userStories.title: MCP server scaffold and transport
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the MCP server entry point wired up with stdio transport so it can be spawned as a child process by opencode.
acceptanceCriteria: mcp-server/index.ts creates an MCP server with name 'ralph-teams' using @modelcontextprotocol/sdk StdioServerTransport
Server starts and responds to MCP initialize handshake
PROJECT_PATH env var is validated on startup — server exits with clear error if missing
package.json in mcp-server/ has build script outputting to dist/
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Read tools: get_prd, get_status, get_logs, validate_prd
userStories.description: As the AI assistant, I need read-only tools to inspect the current state of a project so I can answer user questions and decide what actions to take.
acceptanceCriteria: get_prd tool reads and returns parsed prd.json from PROJECT_PATH
get_status tool returns epic/story pass-fail summary (epic id, title, status, story counts)
get_logs tool reads progress.txt from PROJECT_PATH, accepts optional 'lines' param (default 50), returns last N lines
validate_prd tool runs rjq validate on the prd.json and returns any errors
All tools return structured JSON responses
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Run control tools: run_epics, stop_run, resume_run
userStories.description: As the AI assistant, I need tools to start, stop, and resume ralph-teams runs so I can act on the user's behalf.
acceptanceCriteria: run_epics tool POSTs to runner API /run/:projectId with backend param (default 'claude'), returns runId or error if already running
stop_run tool POSTs to runner API /run/:projectId/stop, returns success/error
resume_run tool POSTs to runner API /run/:projectId/resume, returns success/error if no ralph-state.json
All tools call runner via RUNNER_URL env var
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Edit tools: reset_epic, update_story
userStories.description: As the AI assistant, I need tools to modify the PRD so I can help users adjust their plan mid-run.
acceptanceCriteria: reset_epic tool accepts epicId, uses rjq to set epic status to 'pending' and all its stories passes to false in PROJECT_PATH/prd.json
update_story tool accepts storyId and a fields object (title, description, acceptanceCriteria, passes), applies updates to prd.json using rjq
Both tools validate the id exists before modifying — return clear error if not found
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Git tools: sync_repo, push_results
userStories.description: As the AI assistant, I need git tools to keep the project repo in sync with GitHub.
acceptanceCriteria: sync_repo tool runs git pull origin in PROJECT_PATH, returns stdout/stderr and success flag
push_results tool accepts optional commitMessage and openPr (boolean) params
push_results runs git add -A, git commit, git push in PROJECT_PATH using stored GitHub token from GITHUB_TOKEN env var
If openPr is true, tool calls GitHub API to open a PR from current branch to main and returns PR URL
Typecheck passes

epics.title: Runner Service
epics.description: Express.js service deployed on Hetzner/Coolify (single persistent container). Manages ralph.sh processes (one per project), streams logs to Supabase, syncs project status to Supabase, and hosts the opencode server per chat session. Lives in runner/ directory.

userStories.title: Express server with health check and auth middleware
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the runner Express server set up with a shared secret auth middleware so only the web app can call it.
acceptanceCriteria: runner/index.ts creates Express app on PORT env var (default 3001)
All routes except GET /health require Authorization: Bearer <RUNNER_SECRET> header
GET /health returns { status: 'ok', runningProjects: string[] }
Supabase client initialised with SUPABASE_URL and SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY env vars
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Process registry and run API routes
userStories.description: As the web app, I need to start, stop, and check the status of ralph.sh runs via the runner API.
acceptanceCriteria: In-memory process registry: Map<projectId, { process, runId, startedAt }>
POST /run/:projectId spawns ralph.sh with prd.json in project's local path, inserts run record to Supabase runs table, rejects with 409 if project already running
DELETE /run/:projectId sends SIGINT to process, updates run status to 'stopped'
POST /run/:projectId/resume runs ralph-teams resume in project path
GET /run/:projectId/status returns { status: 'running' | 'idle', runId?, startedAt? }
On process exit, updates run record ended_at and status in Supabase
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Log streamer: pipe ralph.sh output to Supabase
userStories.description: As the web app, I need run logs written to Supabase in real-time so the browser can subscribe via Realtime.
acceptanceCriteria: runner/lib/log-streamer.ts attaches to stdout/stderr of ralph.sh child process
Each line is inserted into Supabase run_logs table with run_id, ts, and line
Inserts are batched (max 50ms delay) to avoid excessive Supabase writes
Log streamer handles process exit cleanly without hanging
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Status syncer: parse prd.json changes to Supabase
userStories.description: As the web app, I need epic and story status synced to Supabase so the dashboard can show live pass/fail state without polling files.
acceptanceCriteria: runner/lib/status-syncer.ts watches prd.json in a project path using fs.watch
On change, reads prd.json and upserts all epic/story statuses to Supabase project_status table
Watcher is started when a run begins and stopped when the process exits
Handles rapid file changes by debouncing (200ms)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Opencode session manager and chat SSE route
userStories.description: As the web app, I need the runner to host opencode server instances and stream chat responses via SSE.
acceptanceCriteria: runner/lib/opencode-manager.ts spawns an opencode server per project using @opencode-ai/sdk createOpencode()
Opencode server configured with MCP server pointing to mcp-server/dist/index.js with PROJECT_PATH set to project local path
GET /chat/:projectId accepts a 'message' query param, creates or reuses opencode session, sends prompt, streams SSE response back
SSE events include text deltas, tool_call, tool_result, and done event types
Opencode servers are reused across messages in the same session (sessionId in query param)
Typecheck passes

epics.title: Supabase Schema and Auth
epics.description: Database schema with RLS policies, and authentication (email/password + GitHub OAuth). This is the foundation the web app and runner both depend on. Includes Next.js auth setup with Supabase SSR client.

userStories.title: Database schema and RLS policies
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Supabase schema created with proper row-level security so users can only access their own data.
acceptanceCriteria: supabase/migrations/001_init.sql creates tables: projects, runs, run_logs, project_status, chat_sessions, chat_messages
projects: id uuid pk, user_id uuid refs auth.users, name text, repo_owner text, repo_name text, repo_url text, local_path text, created_at
runs: id uuid pk, project_id uuid refs projects, status text, started_at, ended_at
run_logs: id bigserial pk, run_id uuid refs runs, ts timestamptz, line text
project_status: project_id+epic_id+story_id composite pk, passes bool, updated_at
chat_sessions: id uuid pk, project_id uuid, created_at
chat_messages: id uuid pk, session_id uuid, role text, content text, created_at
RLS enabled on all tables — users can only read/write rows where project.user_id = auth.uid()
run_logs and project_status inherit access through their parent project
Supabase types generated to web/lib/supabase/types.ts

userStories.title: Email/password auth pages
userStories.description: As a new user, I want to register and log in with email and password.
acceptanceCriteria: web/app/(auth)/login/page.tsx renders email + password form with submit button
web/app/(auth)/register/page.tsx renders email + password + confirm password form
Both use Supabase Auth signInWithPassword and signUp
Form validation: email format, password min 8 chars, passwords match on register
On success: redirect to / (project list)
On error: show inline error message (invalid credentials, email taken, etc.)
Tailwind styled, mobile-first (full-width inputs, large touch targets)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: GitHub OAuth login and token storage
userStories.description: As a user, I want to sign in with GitHub so I can access my repos without entering credentials separately.
acceptanceCriteria: 'Sign in with GitHub' button on login page triggers Supabase signInWithOAuth({ provider: 'github', scopes: 'repo' })
OAuth callback handled at web/app/auth/callback/route.ts — exchanges code for session
GitHub access token extracted from session and stored encrypted in Supabase users metadata (user_metadata.github_token)
Login page shows both email/password form and GitHub OAuth button separated by 'or'
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Auth guard and session middleware
userStories.description: As a developer, I need unauthenticated users redirected to login and authenticated users to have a Supabase session available in all app routes.
acceptanceCriteria: web/middleware.ts uses Supabase SSR to refresh session on every request
Requests to /app/* without a valid session redirect to /login
Requests to /login or /register with a valid session redirect to /
web/lib/supabase/server.ts exports createServerClient helper for use in API routes and server components
web/lib/supabase/client.ts exports createBrowserClient for use in client components
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: User account page and logout
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see my account info and log out.
acceptanceCriteria: web/app/(app)/account/page.tsx shows email, auth provider (email or GitHub), account created date
Logout button calls supabase.auth.signOut() and redirects to /login
Account page accessible from bottom nav or header avatar
Typecheck passes

epics.title: Project Management
epics.description: Users can create projects by linking a GitHub repo, view all their projects, and delete projects. Creating a project clones the repo to the runner's persistent volume via the runner API.

userStories.title: Project list page
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see all my projects on the home screen with their current run status.
acceptanceCriteria: web/app/(app)/page.tsx fetches projects from Supabase for the authenticated user
Each project shown as a card with: name, repo (owner/name), run status badge (running=green dot, idle=gray dot)
Run status fetched from GET /run/:projectId/status on runner
Empty state: 'No projects yet' with a prominent Create Project button
Cards are tappable and navigate to /projects/[id]
Mobile-first card grid (1 column on mobile, 2 on tablet+)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: GitHub repo picker
userStories.description: As a user creating a project, I want to browse and select one of my GitHub repos.
acceptanceCriteria: web/app/(app)/projects/new/page.tsx shows a searchable list of user's GitHub repos
Repos fetched from GET /api/github/repos which calls GitHub API using stored github_token
List shows repo name, visibility (public/private), and last updated date
Search filters by repo name client-side
Selecting a repo populates project name (editable) and proceeds to confirmation
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create project and clone repo
userStories.description: As a user, I want to create a project which clones the selected GitHub repo to the runner so ralph-teams can work on it.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/projects inserts project record to Supabase and calls runner POST /projects with { projectId, repoUrl, githubToken }
Runner POST /projects handler clones repo to /repos/<projectId>/ using git clone with token-authenticated URL
Runner returns local_path which is stored in Supabase projects.local_path
On success: redirect to /projects/[id]
Show loading state during clone (can take a few seconds)
If clone fails: show error and do not insert project record (rollback)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Delete project
userStories.description: As a user, I want to delete a project to remove it from my list and clean up runner files.
acceptanceCriteria: Delete option accessible from project settings or project card long-press
Confirmation dialog before deletion
DELETE /api/projects/[id] stops any running process, calls runner DELETE /projects/:projectId to remove /repos/<projectId>/, deletes Supabase project record (cascades to runs, logs, status)
After delete: redirect to / with success toast
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Project settings page
userStories.description: As a user, I want to view and update my project's details.
acceptanceCriteria: web/app/(app)/projects/[id]/settings/page.tsx shows project name (editable), linked repo URL, local path on runner, created date
Save button updates project name in Supabase
Sync repo button calls POST /api/projects/[id]/sync which calls runner POST /sync/:projectId (git pull), shows output in a log box
Delete project button (red, with confirmation) as described in US-019
Typecheck passes

epics.title: Dashboard and Run Controls
epics.description: Per-project dashboard showing epic/story status, run controls (start/stop/resume), and a live log stream. This is the main operational view of the app.

userStories.title: Epic status cards
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see the status of all epics and stories in my project at a glance.
acceptanceCriteria: web/app/(app)/projects/[id]/page.tsx fetches project_status from Supabase for all epics/stories
Each epic rendered as a card with: title, status badge (pending/running/completed/partial/failed), story progress dots (filled=passed, empty=not passed)
Story count shown as 'X/Y passed'
Tapping an epic card expands it to show individual story rows with pass/fail icons
Cards subscribe to Supabase Realtime on project_status table — update live as runner syncs status
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Run controls: start, stop, resume
userStories.description: As a user, I want to start, stop, and resume ralph-teams runs from the dashboard.
acceptanceCriteria: Run button (▶) shown when project is idle — calls POST /api/projects/[id]/run
Stop button (■) shown when project is running — calls DELETE /api/projects/[id]/run with confirmation dialog
Resume button shown when ralph-state.json exists (check via GET /run/:projectId/status returning canResume: true) — calls POST /api/projects/[id]/run/resume
Backend selector (claude/copilot/codex) shown as a small pill toggle before starting
Button states update immediately optimistically then confirm via status poll
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Live log stream
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see ralph.sh log output in real-time while a run is active.
acceptanceCriteria: web/app/(app)/projects/[id]/logs/page.tsx shows a scrollable log terminal
On load, fetches last 200 lines from Supabase run_logs for the latest run
Subscribes to Supabase Realtime on run_logs filtered by run_id — appends new lines as they arrive
Auto-scrolls to bottom on new lines (unless user has manually scrolled up)
Log lines colorized: lines containing 'PASS' in green, 'FAIL' or 'ERROR' in red, 'WARN' in yellow
Monospace font, dark background (terminal aesthetic)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Run history
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see past runs for a project so I can review what happened.
acceptanceCriteria: Run history section on project dashboard (collapsed by default, expandable)
Lists last 10 runs from Supabase runs table: started_at, ended_at, status badge, duration
Tapping a run shows its logs (same log view but for that run_id)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Push results to GitHub
userStories.description: As a user, I want to push committed changes from a run back to GitHub.
acceptanceCriteria: Push button shown on dashboard after a run completes
Calls POST /api/projects/[id]/push with optional { openPr: boolean }
API route calls runner POST /push/:projectId which runs git push using stored GitHub token
If openPr: true, opens a PR and returns URL — show PR URL as a tappable link
Show success toast with branch name and commit count pushed
Show error if push fails (e.g. conflicts) with git output
Typecheck passes

epics.title: PRD Editor
epics.description: In-app editor for viewing and modifying the project's prd.json. Users can add/edit/remove epics and stories, reset epics, and validate the PRD — all without leaving the web app.

userStories.title: PRD viewer: epics and stories list
userStories.description: As a user, I want to view the full PRD structure in the app.
acceptanceCriteria: web/app/(app)/projects/[id]/prd/page.tsx fetches prd.json via GET /api/projects/[id]/prd
API route reads prd.json from project local_path via runner GET /projects/:projectId/prd
Renders accordion list: epics expandable to show stories
Each story shows title, priority badge, passes status icon, and acceptance criteria count
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Edit story fields
userStories.description: As a user, I want to edit a story's title, description, and acceptance criteria in a mobile-friendly form.
acceptanceCriteria: Tapping a story opens a bottom sheet (mobile) or modal (tablet+) with an edit form
Form fields: title (text input), description (textarea), acceptance criteria (multi-line list with add/remove per item)
Save calls PATCH /api/projects/[id]/stories/[storyId] which calls runner PATCH /projects/:projectId/stories/:storyId
Runner handler uses rjq to update story fields in prd.json
Optimistic UI update: form closes and list updates before server confirms
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add and remove epics and stories
userStories.description: As a user, I want to add new epics/stories and remove existing ones from the PRD.
acceptanceCriteria: Add Epic button opens a form: title, description, dependsOn (multiselect of existing epic IDs)
Add Story button within an epic opens a form: title, description, acceptance criteria (at least one required)
New IDs auto-generated as next EPIC-### and US-### using rjq length queries
Remove epic/story: swipe-to-delete on mobile, delete icon on desktop — confirmation dialog
All mutations call runner API which uses rjq to modify prd.json
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Reset epic from UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want to reset a failed epic back to pending so I can re-run it.
acceptanceCriteria: Reset option in epic's action menu (three-dot menu on epic card)
Calls POST /api/projects/[id]/epics/[epicId]/reset which calls runner POST /projects/:projectId/epics/:epicId/reset
Runner uses rjq to set epic status to 'pending' and all stories passes to false
PRD viewer re-fetches after reset and shows updated state
Action disabled if a run is currently active (show tooltip: 'Stop the run first')
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Validate PRD and show errors inline
userStories.description: As a user, I want to validate my PRD and see any errors highlighted directly in the editor.
acceptanceCriteria: Validate button in PRD editor header calls POST /api/projects/[id]/prd/validate
API calls runner which runs ralph-teams validate and returns structured error list
Errors shown inline: epics or stories with issues highlighted with red border and error tooltip
Success state: green checkmark with 'PRD is valid' message
Validation also runs automatically before starting a run — blocks run start if invalid
Typecheck passes

epics.title: AI Chat
epics.description: Per-project AI chat using opencode SDK (proxied through runner). The assistant has access to all MCP tools and can operate ralph-teams on behalf of the user. Chat history persisted in Supabase.

userStories.title: Chat page UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want a clean mobile-optimized chat interface for talking to the AI assistant.
acceptanceCriteria: web/app/(app)/projects/[id]/chat/page.tsx renders a full-height chat interface
Message thread scrollable, pinned to bottom on new messages
User messages right-aligned (blue bubble), assistant messages left-aligned (gray bubble)
Text input fixed at bottom with send button, submit on Enter (Shift+Enter for newline)
Tool call events shown as collapsed pills between messages: '🔧 get_status' expandable to show input/output
Loading indicator (animated dots) while assistant is responding
Chat history loaded from Supabase chat_messages on page load
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Chat SSE streaming via runner proxy
userStories.description: As the chat UI, I need assistant responses streamed in real-time from the opencode server on the runner.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/projects/[id]/chat sends message + sessionId to runner GET /chat/:projectId?message=...&sessionId=...
Next.js API route proxies runner SSE stream to browser as a ReadableStream response
Browser EventSource or fetch with ReadableStream reader processes: text_delta, tool_call, tool_result, done event types
Text deltas appended to current assistant message bubble in real-time
On done event: full message saved to Supabase chat_messages
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Chat session management
userStories.description: As a user, I want to start new chat sessions and access recent ones.
acceptanceCriteria: New Chat button creates a new chat_sessions record in Supabase and clears the message thread
Session list accessible via a sessions drawer (swipe from left on mobile)
Drawer shows last 10 sessions with first message preview and date
Tapping a session loads its messages from Supabase chat_messages
Current sessionId passed to runner on every message so opencode maintains context
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Assistant system prompt and context injection
userStories.description: As the AI assistant, I need project context injected into my system prompt so I can give relevant, accurate responses.
acceptanceCriteria: Runner injects system prompt into opencode session on creation: project name, repo URL, current epic/story status summary, available MCP tools list
System prompt instructs assistant to always check get_status before answering questions about progress
System prompt instructs assistant not to run_epics without explicit user confirmation
System prompt format defined in runner/lib/opencode-manager.ts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Suggested prompts and quick actions
userStories.description: As a user, I want suggested prompts when starting a chat so I know what I can ask.
acceptanceCriteria: Empty chat state shows 4 suggested prompt chips: 'What's the current status?', 'Start a new run', 'Why did the last run fail?', 'Reset failed epics'
Tapping a chip sends that prompt immediately
Chips disappear once first message is sent
Typecheck passes

epics.title: Mobile Polish and PWA
epics.description: Mobile-first UI polish pass: bottom navigation, PWA support, loading/error/empty states, touch interactions, and responsive layout across all pages.

userStories.title: Bottom navigation
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want a bottom navigation bar to switch between the main sections of a project.
acceptanceCriteria: web/components/BottomNav.tsx renders a fixed bottom bar with 4 tabs: Dashboard, PRD, Chat, Logs
Active tab highlighted, uses Next.js usePathname to detect current route
Navigation bar only visible within /projects/[id]/* routes
Top-level / (project list) has its own header with account avatar linking to /account
Tab icons from lucide-react (LayoutDashboard, FileText, MessageSquare, ScrollText)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: PWA manifest and icons
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want to add the app to my home screen for a native-like experience.
acceptanceCriteria: web/public/manifest.json with name 'Ralph', short_name 'Ralph', start_url '/', display 'standalone', theme_color and background_color
App icons at 192x192 and 512x512 in web/public/icons/
web/app/layout.tsx includes manifest link and apple-touch-icon meta tags
Viewport meta tag set for mobile: width=device-width, initial-scale=1, maximum-scale=1
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Loading skeletons and error boundaries
userStories.description: As a user, I want clear loading and error states on every page so the app never feels broken.
acceptanceCriteria: Every page that fetches data has a loading.tsx with skeleton UI matching the page layout
Every page has an error.tsx that shows a friendly error message with a Retry button
Supabase fetch errors display toast notifications (use a simple toast component, not a library)
Runner API errors (5xx, network) shown as inline error banners with retry
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Touch interactions and responsive layout
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want all interactions to feel native with proper touch targets and responsive layouts.
acceptanceCriteria: All tappable elements minimum 44x44px touch target
Project cards on home page: 1 column on mobile (<640px), 2 columns on tablet (640px+)
Epic cards on dashboard: full width on mobile, 2 columns on desktop (1024px+)
Bottom sheet component used for edit forms on mobile (slides up from bottom, dismissable by dragging down)
No horizontal overflow on any page at 375px viewport width
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Empty states and onboarding flow
userStories.description: As a new user, I want clear guidance on what to do next at every empty state.
acceptanceCriteria: Project list empty state: illustration + 'Create your first project' button
PRD editor empty state (no epics): 'Your prd.json is empty. Ask the AI to help you create one.' with a button linking to Chat
Log view empty state (no run yet): 'No runs yet. Start a run from the Dashboard.'
Chat empty state: as described in US-035 suggested prompts
All empty states are centered vertically, mobile-friendly, with helpful copy
Typecheck passes
